coredata.dc:title: High-yield and Eco-Friendly Fabrication of Ultra-Long (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O Nanowires and Their Electrochemistry Performances
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2017 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Liu, Yaning
Shen, Bolei
Liu, Xin
Wu, Yi
He, Xiangfei
Li, Qing
coredata.dc:description: Ultra-long (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O nanowires are synthesized in high-yield by a chitosan (CTS) assisted hydrothermal approach at 180 °C for 24 h. The as-prepared materials are characterized by various characterization methods. The experimental results reveal that the products are pure (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O single crystals with a width of 200-300 nm and a length of more than 150 μm. Such ultra-long (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O nanowires present great lithium-ion transmission ability, high electrochemical storage capacity. The nanowires deliver initial discharge specific capacity of 183.6 mAh g-1 at 50 mA g-1. After 50 cycles, they still retained discharge specific capacity of 161.6 mAh g-1, exhibiting good cyclic stability.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Ultra-long (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O nanowires
hydrothermal approach
electrochemical performance
Li-ion batteries

xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2017 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: High-yield and Eco-Friendly Fabrication of Ultra-Long (NH4 ) )2 V V6 O O16 Â·1.5H Â·1.5H2 O Nanowires and Their Electrochemistry PerformancesYaning Liu, Bolei Shen, Xin Liu, Yi Wu, Xiangfei He, Qing Li, qli@swu.edu.cn Faculty of Materials and Energy, Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, China. Faculty of Materials and Energy Southwest University Chongqing 400715 China Faculty of Materials and Energy, Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, China. Ultra-long (NH4 ) )2 V V6 O O16 Â·1.5H Â·1.5H2 O nanowires are synthesized in high-yield by a chitosan (CTS) assisted hydrothermal approach at 180 Â°C for 24 h. The as-prepared materials are characterized by various characterization methods. The experimental results reveal that the products are pure (NH O nanowires are synthesized in high-yield by a chitosan (CTS) assisted hydrothermal approach at 180 Â°C for 24 h. The as-prepared materials are characterized by various characterization methods. The experimental results reveal that the products are pure (NH4 ) )2 V V6 O O16 Â·1.5H Â·1.5H2 O single crystals with a width of 200-300 nm and a length of more than 150 Î¼m. Such ultra-long (NH O single crystals with a width of 200-300 nm and a length of more than 150 Î¼m. Such ultra-long (NH4 ) )2 V V6 O O16 Â·1.5H Â·1.5H2 O nanowires present great lithium-ion transmission ability, high electrochemical storage capacity. The nanowires deliver initial discharge specific capacity of 183.6 mAh g O nanowires present great lithium-ion transmission ability, high electrochemical storage capacity. The nanowires deliver initial discharge specific capacity of 183.6 mAh g-1 at 50 mA g at 50 mA g-1 . After 50 cycles, they still retained discharge specific capacity of 161.6 mAh g . After 50 cycles, they still retained discharge specific capacity of 161.6 mAh g-1 , exhibiting good cyclic stability.Keywords Ultra-long (NH4 ) )2 V V6 O O16 Â·1.5H Â·1.5H2 O nanowireshydrothermal approach electrochemical performance Li-ion batteries References 1. simple-head.ce:title: High-yield and Eco-Friendly Fabrication of Ultra-Long (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O Nanowires and Their Electrochemistry Performances

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Faculty of Materials and Energy, Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, China.
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Faculty of Materials and Energy, Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, China.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Ultra-long (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O nanowires are synthesized in high-yield by a chitosan (CTS) assisted hydrothermal approach at 180 °C for 24 h. The as-prepared materials are characterized by various characterization methods. The experimental results reveal that the products are pure (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O single crystals with a width of 200-300 nm and a length of more than 150 μm. Such ultra-long (NH4)2V6O16·1.5H2O nanowires present great lithium-ion transmission ability, high electrochemical storage capacity. The nanowires deliver initial discharge specific capacity of 183.6 mAh g-1 at 50 mA g-1. After 50 cycles, they still retained discharge specific capacity of 161.6 mAh g-1, exhibiting good cyclic stability.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: S. Y. Lee, K. H. Choi, W. S. Choi, Y. H. Kwon, H. R. Jung, H. C. Shin, J. Y. Kim, Energy Environ. Sci., 6 (2013) 2414.
